seoTitle: Mount Stuart vs Mount Sturt — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Stuart and Mount Sturt. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Stuart vs Mount Sturt: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Stuart and Mount Sturt.
heading: Mount Stuart vs Mount Sturt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Stuart, Queensland and Mount Sturt, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Stuart has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Sturt (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Stuart or Mount Sturt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Stuart has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Mount Sturt scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Stuart scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Sturt scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Stuart or Mount Sturt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Stuart has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
